title: विद्या प्राप्ति स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विद्या प्राप्ति स्तोत्रम् ॥

सरस्वति नमस्तुभ्यं वरदे कामरूपिणि।
विद्यारम्भं करिष्यामि सिद्धिर्भवतु मे सदा॥ १॥

या कुन्देन्दुतुषारहारधवला या शुभ्रवस्त्रावृता
या वीणावरदण्डमण्डितकरा या श्वेतपद्मासना।
या ब्रह्माच्युतशंकरप्रभृतिभिर्देवैः सदा पूजिता
सा मां पातु सरस्वती भगवती निःशेषजाड्यापहा॥ २॥

शुक्लां ब्रह्मविचारसारपरमां आद्यां जगद्व्यापिनीं
वीणापुस्तकधारिणीमभयदां जाड्यान्धकारापहाम्।
हस्ते स्फाटिकमालिकां च दधतीं पद्मासने संस्थितां
वन्दे तां परमेश्वरीं भगवतीं बुद्धिप्रदां शारदाम्॥ ३॥

विद्याधिष्ठातृदेवि त्वं विद्यां देहि नमोऽस्तु ते।
मेधां प्रज्ञां च धारणां स्मृतिं च देहि शारदे॥ ४॥

सरस्वत्यै नमो नित्यं भद्रकाल्यै नमो नमः।
वेदवेदान्तवेदाङ्गविद्यास्थानेभ्य एव च॥ ५॥

महाविद्या महामाया महामेधा महास्मृतिः।
महामोहा च भगवती महादेवी महेश्वरी॥ ६॥

पद्मपत्रविशालाक्षि पद्मकेसरवर्णिनि।
नित्यं पद्मालया देवि सा मां पातु सरस्वती॥ ७॥

कलिकल्मषनाशिन्यै विद्यादायिन्यै नमो नमः।
प्रसीद भगवत्यम्ब विद्यां देहि च मेधया॥ ८॥

वाग्देवि त्वं प्रसीदस्व विद्यां बुद्धिं च देहि मे।
रत्नपूर्णां सरस्वत्याः कृपां कुरु सदा मयि॥ ९॥

॥ इति श्री विद्या प्राप्ति स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vidya Prapti Stotram ||

Sarasvati namastubhyam varade kamarupini |
Vidyarambham karishyami siddhir-bhavatu me sada || 1 ||

Ya kundendu-tushara-hara-dhavala ya shubhra-vastravrita
ya veena-vara-danda-mandita-kara ya shveta-padmasana |
Ya Brahma-Achyuta-Shankara-prabhritibhir-devaih sada pujita
sa mam patu Sarasvati Bhagavati nihshesha-jadya-apaha || 2 ||

Shuklam Brahma-vichara-sara-paramam adyam jagad-vyapinim
veena-pustaka-dharinim-abhaya-dam jadya-andhakara-apaham |
Haste sphatika-malikam cha dadhatim padmasane samsthitam
vande tam Parameshvarim Bhagavatim buddhi-pradam Sharadam || 3 ||

Vidya-adhishtatri-devi tvam vidyam dehi namo'stu te |
Medham prajnam cha dharanam smritim cha dehi Sharade || 4 ||

Sarasvatyai namo nityam Bhadrakalyai namo namah |
Veda-vedanta-vedanga-vidya-sthanebhya eva cha || 5 ||

Maha-vidya Maha-maya Maha-medha Maha-smritih |
Maha-moha cha Bhagavati Maha-Devi Maheshvari || 6 ||

Padma-patra-vishalakshi padma-kesara-varnini |
Nityam padmalaya Devi sa mam patu Sarasvati || 7 ||

Kali-kalmasha-nashin-yai vidya-dayin-yai namo namah |
Prasida Bhagavaty-amba vidyam dehi cha medhaya || 8 ||

Vag-Devi tvam prasidhasva vidyam buddhim cha dehi me |
Ratna-purnam Sarasvatyah kripam kuru sada mayi || 9 ||

|| Iti Shri Vidya Prapti Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
हे सरस्वती! तुम्हें नमस्कार, वरदायिनी कामरूपिणी। मैं विद्या का आरम्भ करता हूँ, मुझे सदा सिद्धि प्राप्त हो॥ १॥

जो कुन्द, चन्द्र और तुषार हार के समान धवल हैं, शुभ्र वस्त्र धारण करती हैं, वीणा और वरदण्ड से मण्डित हैं, श्वेत पद्म पर आसीन हैं, ब्रह्मा, विष्णु, शंकर आदि देवों द्वारा सदा पूजित हैं — वे सरस्वती भगवती समस्त जड़ता को हरने वाली मेरी रक्षा करें॥ २॥

शुक्लवर्णा, ब्रह्मविचार की सारभूत परम आद्या, जगद्व्यापिनी, वीणा-पुस्तक धारिणी, अभयदायिनी, जड़ता के अन्धकार को दूर करने वाली, स्फटिक माला धारण करती, पद्मासन पर स्थित, बुद्धिप्रदा शारदा परमेश्वरी भगवती को मैं वन्दन करता हूँ॥ ३॥

हे विद्या की अधिष्ठात्री देवी! मुझे विद्या दो, आपको नमस्कार। हे शारदे! मेधा, प्रज्ञा, धारणा और स्मृति प्रदान करो॥ ४॥

सरस्वती को नित्य नमस्कार, भद्रकाली को नमस्कार। वेद, वेदान्त, वेदाङ्ग और विद्यास्थानों को नमन॥ ५॥

महाविद्या, महामाया, महामेधा, महास्मृति, महामोहा, भगवती, महादेवी, महेश्वरी॥ ६॥

पद्मपत्र के समान विशाल नेत्रों वाली, पद्म-केसर वर्ण वाली, नित्य पद्म में निवास करने वाली देवी सरस्वती मेरी रक्षा करें॥ ७॥

कलिकल्मष का नाश करने वाली, विद्या देने वाली को नमस्कार। हे भगवती अम्बा! प्रसन्न हों, मेधा सहित विद्या प्रदान करें॥ ८॥

हे वाग्देवी! प्रसन्न हों, मुझे विद्या और बुद्धि दें। सरस्वती की रत्नपूर्ण कृपा मुझ पर सदा बनी रहे॥ ९॥
benefits: Invokes the blessing of divine knowledge and sharp intellect
Enhances memory, concentration, and retention power
Traditionally recited for removal of intellectual dullness and ignorance
Blesses students with success in examinations
Invokes eloquence and mastery over speech
Ideal for beginning any course of study
description: Vidya Prapti Stotram (विद्या प्राप्ति स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Goddess Saraswati. Attributed to Saraswati Stotram - Traditional, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to enhance memory, concentration, and retention power as well as remove intellectual dullness and ignorance. Additionally, it is known to bless students with success in examinations. For best results, devotees chant this on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Vasant Panchami and Saraswati Puja. At 9 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 6 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Vidya Prapti Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vidya Prapti Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. A powerful Saraswati prayer for gaining knowledge, sharp memory, and success in education.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Vidya Prapti Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vidya Prapti Stotram (literally the hymn for the attainment of knowledge) is a Sanskrit prayer composition addressed to Devi Saraswati for success in learning, examinations, debates, oratory and all forms of vidya. It opens with the foundational invocation Saraswati namastubhyam varade kama-rupini / Vidyarambham karishyami siddhir-bhavatu me sada — Salutations to you, O Saraswati, the giver of boons and the assumer of all desired forms; I commence my study, may there always be success for me. This is followed by the celebrated Yaa Kundendu Tushara Hara Dhavala verse and additional supplications for memory, intellect, eloquence and elimination of dullness. It is preserved in classical tradition and recited universally by Hindu students before commencing study. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi vidya stotras; classical Indian gurukula tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the opening verse Saraswati Namastubhyam mean?
seoFaqs.answer: Saraswati namastubhyam varade kama-rupini / Vidyarambham karishyami siddhir-bhavatu me sada is the most universally recited Saraswati shloka in Hindu households, schools and traditional gurukulas. Word by word: Saraswati — O Saraswati; namah tubhyam — salutations to you; varade — O giver of boons; kama-rupini — O assumer of any desired form; vidyarambham karishyami — I am beginning my study; siddhih bhavatu — let there be success; me — for me; sada — always. It is recited by every student of Sanskrit before opening a new book, by children at the akshara-abhyasam (first writing) initiation, and by devotees before commencing any study, exam, debate, public speaking or creative composition. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; classical gurukula tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Vidya Prapti Stotram traditionally chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: The principal occasions are Vidyarambha day (often the Vijayadashami of Sharad Navaratri or the Vasant Panchami of Magha Shukla Panchami), when children are formally initiated into letters and learning by writing the first akshara (often Om, Hari Sri or the child's name) in rice or sandalwood paste under a guru's hand. It is also chanted daily at the start of study sessions, before examinations, before debates, before public lectures, and at the opening of school assemblies. In traditional gurukulas it is the prarthana shloka chanted before each daily Veda-adhyayana session. Many South Indian schools begin the morning assembly with this stotram in place of or alongside the national anthem prayer. (Sources: Drikpanchang Vasant Panchami; Vidyarambha vrat manuals.)

seoFaqs.question: What is Vidyarambha and what is its connection to this stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Vidyarambha (literally beginning of learning) is the Hindu samskara that initiates a child into formal education, traditionally performed between ages two and five, most often on Vijayadashami or Vasant Panchami. The child is bathed, dressed in new clothes, and seated facing east before an image of Saraswati and Ganesha. The guru or family elder holds the child's hand and writes the first letters in rice — typically Om, Hari Sri Ganapataye Namah, or in Tamil tradition simply the alphabet — while the Vidya Prapti Stotram is recited. The child then writes on a banana leaf with a kajal stick. This samskara, considered foundational for academic life, formally begins the child's lifelong relationship with Saraswati. (Sources: Sixteen Samskaras texts; Drikpanchang Vidyarambha vrat.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does Vidya Prapti Stotram promise to students?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram promises siddhi (success) in all academic and intellectual endeavours: success in examinations and competitions, eloquence in oral debates and presentations, sharpness of memory, dispelling of dullness and distraction, removal of speech defects such as stammering, capacity for sustained study, deep comprehension of difficult texts, ability to compose poetry and prose, and ultimately the supreme jnana that liberates from the cycle of ignorance. For working professionals it grants success in interviews, public speaking, writing and creative pursuits. For musicians, dancers and artists it grants kala-siddhi (perfection in their art). The repeated chant siddhir-bhavatu me sada — let there always be success for me — anchors the entire prayer. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi phala-shruti; traditional gurukula commentaries.)

seoFaqs.question: How should students chant the Vidya Prapti Stotram for best results?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional practice prescribes a bath, clean clothes and a clean study seat (preferably facing east), with a small altar holding an image or yantra of Saraswati, books, pens and a coconut. The student lights a ghee lamp and an agarbatti, offers white flowers, and chants the stotram aloud at least three times before commencing study. During examination seasons the recitation is intensified to eleven, twenty-one or one hundred and eight times daily, often combined with the Saraswati beej mantra Aim Saraswatyai Namah. On Vasant Panchami and the day before major examinations, students traditionally place their textbooks at the feet of a Saraswati murti and recite the stotram before retrieving them for study. (Sources: Vasant Panchami vrat manuals; traditional Hindu student practice.)

seoFaqs.question: How is Vidya Prapti Stotram different from Saraswati Stotram and Saraswati Vandana?
seoFaqs.answer: All three texts are Saraswati-centred prayers but differ in scope and use. The Saraswati Vandana is the briefest, often a single shloka such as Yaa Kundendu Tushara Hara Dhavala, recited at school assemblies and at the start of academic functions. The Saraswati Stotram is a longer Sanskrit hymn of multiple verses praising her iconography and cosmic role, used in daily puja and during the Saraswati festivals. The Vidya Prapti Stotram is specifically focused on the goal of vidya-siddhi for students, with the framing prayer Vidyarambham karishyami siddhir-bhavatu me sada, and is the standard prayer chanted at the start of study sessions and at vidyarambha samskara. The three are often recited together — Vandana at school, Stotram at puja, Vidya Prapti at study. (Sources: Stotra Samhita; classical gurukula tradition.)
